source_jobs.title: Consultant Pain Management PAYE
source_jobs.description:
An opportunity to contribute to the future of pain care

Chronic pain remains one of the most complex and challenging areas of medicine, particularly for patients who have reached the limits of conventional treatment.

We are looking for Consultants specialising in Pain Management who are open to exploring cannabis based medicines within a structured, evidence led clinical environment.

Working within a multidisciplinary model, you will focus on patient assessment, prescribing decisions, and ongoing care, while pharmacists support the wider treatment pathway. As this field continues to develop, you will also have the opportunity to contribute to how these treatments are delivered and understood in the UK.



About the role

We are seeking Consultants specialising in Pain Management to join our clinical team. You will play a key role in assessing and managing patients exploring medical cannabis treatment options. This role offers the opportunity to shape the future of medical cannabis treatment in the UK within a collaborative, multidisciplinary environment.



What you will be doing:

 - Provide high quality care for patients seeking medical cannabis treatment.

 - Conduct patient consultations, including assessment and ongoing management.

 - Engage in clinical outcome data collection to support evidence based practices.

 - Contribute to clinical governance and maintain robust patient care standards.

 - Collaborate effectively within a multidisciplinary team, including nurses and pharmacists.

 - Participate in ongoing training and professional development, including bespoke specialist training in cannabis based medicines (CBMPs).

What you need:

 - Full GMC Registration

 - Completion of Specialist Training (CCST/CCT) or equivalent certification

 - On the GMC Specialist Register for Pain Management

 - Demonstrated interest in alternative therapies

 - Willingness to participate in data collection and reporting

Why join us?

 - Highly Competitive Salary: £190,000 - £210,000 FTE, pro rata for part time roles

 - Flexible Working: Full time or part time options available

 - Comprehensive Indemnity Coverage: Montu will cover all professional and clinical indemnity insurance for your work with us

 - Private Healthcare

 - Work-Life Balance: No night shifts or on call requirements

 - Cutting Edge Training: Access to CPD accredited medical cannabis education and specialist in house training from industry leaders

 - Collaborative Environment: Join a multidisciplinary team of healthcare professionals

 - Personal Growth: Be part of a pioneering field and shape the future of medical cannabis care in the UK

Training & Development:

 - Attendance at a recognised Medical Cannabis Course for Specialist Physicians (sponsored by Montu)

 - Specialist training delivered by experts, including Prof Anup Mathew (Consultant Psychiatrist) and Dr Gerard Sinovich (Pain Management Consultant).

 - Ongoing support and training on clinical protocols, prescribing practices, and Montu’s system usage

Working arrangements:

 - Remote Role: Flexible working hours and days

 - Session Structure:

 - Standard session length: 4 hours

 - Appointment duration: 15 minutes (new and existing patients)

Montu’s multidisciplinary model allows consultants to focus on prescribing and patient care, while nurses and pharmacists handle pre consultation and follow up tasks.

If you are passionate about innovative patient care, thrive in a dynamic environment, and are looking to make a real impact in the field of medical cannabis, we would love to hear from you!

Sessions:

Each standard session consists of 4 hours of patient consultations. Clinicians can choose their preferred working days and hours, with sessions spread flexibly if needed.

To support onboarding, clinicians are required to commit to a minimum of 2 sessions per week/ 8 hours initially, to ensure they become proficient with Montu’s platform.

Appointment Durations:

Appointments are 15 minutes

source_jobs.title: Part Time Clinical Pharmacist - Evening & Weekends
source_jobs.description:
About the role:

We are recruiting a Clinical Pharmacist (Independent Prescriber) to join our London team. This is a role, you will work 2-3 days (including the evenings and weekends) in our London office located in Kings Cross. You will play a key part in prescribing and reviewing cannabis based medicines for patients with complex conditions.

The role requires on-site working during evenings and weekends



As a Clinical Pharmacist (Independent Prescriber) you will split your time between three days on site and two days working remotely. You will provide safe, effective and personalised treatment, supporting patients with a wide range of needs.

Collaboration is central to this role. Working alongside a multidisciplinary team, you will help deliver outstanding patient care while also contributing to the development of clinical pathways. You will be involved in patient and staff education and play an active part in driving improvements in treatment outcomes within this rapidly growing field.

Key responsibilities:

As an On-Site Clinical Pharmacist, you will:

• Clinically check and review CBPM prescriptions for safety and accuracy.

• Prescribe medicines as an Independent Prescriber, tailoring treatments to individual patient needs.

• Provide expert guidance to prescribers and patients on treatment options.

• Maintain accurate patient records in line with GPhC standards.

• Support the dispensary team by handling complex clinical queries.

• Deliver education and training on CBPM to patients, clinicians, and staff.

• Collaborate with the wider team to streamline processes and improve outcomes.



What we are looking for:

 - GPhC registered pharmacist with an Independent Prescriber (IP) qualification

 - At least 2 years prescribing experience

 - Experience working in a clinical setting

 - Knowledge of CBPM is desired not essential or willingness to undergo specialised training

 - Clear and confident communicator across clinical and non-clinical settings

 - Highly effective at managing time and prioritising competing demands

 - Proactive and resourceful when identifying and resolving problems

 - Well-organised, detail-oriented, and able to manage complex workflows



What we offer:

 - Generous Leave: 25 days holiday (rising to 27 after year one and 30 after year three) + 8 bank holidays

 - Pension Matching: Up to 5% employer matching contributions

 - Private Healthcare Scheme

 - Flexibility and Wellness: Work-from-home options, cycle-to-work scheme, and more

 - Growth Opportunities: Collaborate across teams and represent Montu at events, with support to grow your skills and impact

 - Enhanced Maternity & Paternity Leave

 

About Montu

Montu UK is a leading digital health company specialising in cannabis-based medicines (CBPM). We are committed to transforming lives by improving access to safe, effective treatments and offering an exceptional standard of care. Our dynamic and supportive work environment is the perfect place for you to grow professionally while making a meaningful impact on patients’ lives.

If you have any questions about this role, please contact our Talent team recruitment@montu.uk

source_jobs.title: General Practitioner - 4 Day Week
source_jobs.description:
What is the job?

An exciting opportunity to be part of a fast scaling health tech company that’s redefining how patients access care.

We are looking for an ambitious General Practitioner to join our growing team and help shape the future of personalised medicine.

This is more than a clinical role, it’s an opportunity to combine hands-on patient care with innovation, education, and leadership in one of the UK’s most exciting areas of healthcare.



What will you be doing?

 - Conduct video-based new patient assessments to evaluate medical suitability for treatment working closely with the head of clinical services, independent prescribing pharmacists, nurses and consultants.

 - Take thorough medical histories and apply strong clinical judgment to guide care decisions in a time-efficient manner.

 - Act as an escalation point for complex safeguarding issues and help guide safeguarding processes alongside our governance team.

 - Provide advice for complex clinical queries.

 - Contribute to training and upskilling our clinical teams.

 - Ensure accurate and compliant patient records in line with governance protocols.

 - Provide patients with clear, supportive guidance on next steps in their treatment journey.

 - Collaborate across a multidisciplinary team to deliver safe prescribing pathways.

 - Contribute to the development of innovative patient pathways and clinical processes.

What do you need?

 - GMC-registered GP with license to practice in the UK.

 - At least 5 years’ post-registration experience.

 - Strong diagnostic and decision-making skills.

 - Comfortable working autonomously in digital-first and hybrid environments.

 - A genuine interest in innovation, health tech, and emerging therapies.

 - Excellent communication skills and digital fluency.

 - Experience in telemedicine or digital health (desirable)

 - Background in pain management, neurology, mental health, or complex chronic conditions (desirable).



What we offer:

 - Generous Leave: 25 days holiday (rising to 27 after year one and 30 after year two) + 8 bank holidays

 - Pension Matching: Up to 5% employer matching contributions

 - Flexibility and Wellness: Work-from-home options, cycle-to-work scheme, and more

Montu UK is a leading digital health company specialising in cannabis-based medicines (CBPM), dedicated to improving patient access to safe and effective treatments. source_jobs.title: Web Developer - Webflow
source_jobs.description:
What is the job?

We are looking for a strategic, creative, and hands-on Web Developer who is focussed on Website Growth to take ownership of the development, optimisation, and performance of our digital presence across Montu UK and Alternaleaf.

This is not a traditional developer role focused solely on building pages. We are looking for someone who can build, experiment, and optimise. You will combine deep Webflow and Wix expertise with a strong understanding of user journeys, SEO, and conversion rate optimisation to continuously improve how our websites perform.

Working closely with marketing, pharmacy, product, and growth teams, you will play a key role in ensuring our websites are high-performing, scalable, and conversion-focused.

What will you be doing?

 - Own and execute the website development and optimisation strategy aligned with business and growth goals

 - Build, maintain, and optimise website pages and templates using Webflow and Wix

 - Develop scalable components and implement custom code (HTML, CSS, JavaScript) where required

 - Collaborate with cross-functional teams to deliver a consistent, high-performing user experience

 - Act as the technical owner for all website initiatives, ensuring best-in-class web standards

 - Translate marketing, product, and brand initiatives into seamless on-site user journeys

 - Implement and manage conversion rate optimisation (CRO) initiatives, including A/B and multivariate testing

 - Monitor site performance and user behaviour using tools such as Google Analytics, Hotjar, and other analytics platforms

 - Apply SEO best practices across site structure, metadata, and technical implementation

 - Ensure mobile-first design, accessibility standards, and page speed optimisation across all platforms

 - Identify opportunities to improve conversion funnels, lead generation, upselling, and cross-selling

 - Conduct usability testing and incorporate data-driven improvements to continuously optimise the user experience

 - Collaborate with content, SEO, CRM, and paid media teams to ensure landing pages and campaigns perform effectively

 - Thrive in a fast-paced, high-growth environment, where testing, iteration, and innovation drive results

What do you need?

 - 5+ years' experience in website marketing, product management, or digital strategy.

 - SME with hands-on expertise in Webflow and Wix (must-have).

 - Proficient in HTML/CSS and basic JavaScript (ability to customise components).

 - Strong understanding of web performance optimisation, custom code within Webflow, and structured data (schema) for enhanced SEO.

 - Experience with API integrations and compliance-led tracking.

 - Experience working in regulated environments and balancing compliance with creativity.

 - Strong track record of improving site performance and conversion rates.

 - Familiarity with CMS workflows, UX/UI principles, and web analytics.

 - Ability to plan, deliver, and own web projects end-to-end.


BONUS POINTS FOR

 - Experience in the healthcare or pharmaceutical industry

 - Knowledge of GDPR, data compliance, and secure form handling

 - Experience integrating CRM tools and marketing automation

 - Understanding of personalisation engines and behavioural triggers

 - Comfort with Agile project methodologies



This role will be hired through our Employee of Record in South Africa, MindPearl.



MindPearl Benefits include:

 - Medical insurance paid for by the company. Insurance is with KaeloHealth.

 - Paid Maternity leave for female employees (3months @100% salary)

 - Annual leave of 20 days plus one additional day per year up to 25 days.

 - Provident Fund with Momentum-employee contribution of minimum 6% per month.

source_jobs.title: Pharmacy Technician
source_jobs.description:
About the role:

This is your opportunity to work at the cutting edge of cannabis-based medicines, supporting patients with complex, often life-changing conditions, in a setting that values your precision, your expertise, and your commitment to getting it right.

Join a passionate, forward-thinking team in one of the fastest-growing healthcare organisations in the UK, where no two patients or days are the same. You will have real involvement in the quality and accuracy of care delivered, working alongside pharmacists and clinicians to ensure every patient receives the standard of treatment they deserve.



What you will be doing:

 - Support Clinical Pharmacists by reviewing prescription requests and associated patient documentation prior to prescribing and dispensing.

 - Assess patient records and supporting documentation to ensure eligibility for treatment in line with clinical protocols, regulatory requirements, and internal policies.

 - Identify missing, incomplete, or inconsistent information and escalate queries appropriately to the Clinical Pharmacist team.

 - Maintain accurate and up-to-date patient and prescription records within internal systems.

 - Work collaboratively with pharmacists, clinicians, and patient support teams to deliver a seamless patient experience.

 - Respond to internal queries relating to prescription processing and documentation requirements.

 - Contribute to the continuous improvement of operational processes and quality assurance procedures.

 - Support onboarding and training of junior team members where required.

 - Engage in ongoing professional development and remain up to date with relevant pharmacy regulations and best practice guidance.



What you will need:

 - Hold a Level 3 NVQ in Pharmacy Services (or equivalent qualification).

 - Be registered with the General Pharmaceutical Council (GPhC) as a Pharmacy Technician.

 - Has previous experience working in a pharmacy, primary care, or wider healthcare setting.

 - Possess a strong understanding of pharmacy regulations, governance, and standard operating procedures.

 - Demonstrate exceptional attention to detail and the ability to manage a high volume of work accurately.

 - Be highly organised with strong administrative and documentation review skills.

 - Have excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with a patient-centred approach to care.

 - Be comfortable working in a fast-paced, collaborative, and evolving healthcare environment.

source_jobs.title: Safeguarding and Complex Health Lead
source_jobs.description:
At Montu, we are re transforming access to cannabis-based medicines through safe, evidence-led digital healthcare. We’re looking for an experienced health professional who thrives in complex clinical environments, is passionate about safeguarding and governance, and wants to play a pivotal role in improving outcomes for patients with nuanced and long-term mental health needs.

In this position, you will be a vital bridge between our clinical, governance, safeguarding, and patient-care teams, and will act as our organisational Safeguarding Leads. You will be responsible for the caseload of complex patients, ranging from mental health and those living with long term conditions. You'll be involved in follow-up of these patients while also serving as the internal subject matter expert for clinicians and the wider team.

Your expertise will guide the wider team and ensure our clinical governance frameworks meet the highest standards of safety.

 


WHAT YOU WILL BE DOING

Complex Caseload & Longitudinal Management:

 - Specialist Follow-up Care: Regular review of the management of a dedicated caseload of patients who are already under a mental health team or where this is required. Specifically patients with escalating or intricate needs, ensuring their long-term treatment plans remain safe and effective.

 - External Clinical Liaison: Act as the key point of contact for external GPs or NHS Mental Health Services where required; facilitating a joined-up approach to patient care and ensuring robust information sharing to maintain patient safety.

 - Complex Clinical Reviews: Conduct deep-dive reviews for cases referred to you whose presentation or medical history requires a higher level of clinical scrutiny than routine follow-up.

 - Stabilisation & Escalation: Act as the primary point of intervention for clinical escalation, providing expert advice to patients and staff when a case moves beyond routine care or requires specialist adjustment for complex mental health patients.

Clinical Governance, Safeguarding & Leadership:

 - Clinical Leader: Act as a primary source of expert advice for all clinicians across the organisation, offering guidance on complex mental health queries and best practices as well as training the wider clinical team in this field.

 - Governance Integration: Work as a key link between the frontline clinical team and the Governance Team, reviewing clinical incidents and refining screening protocols to align with clinical, professional and regulatory standards.

 - Safeguarding Lead: Drive organisational safeguarding excellence through oversight of clinical governance frameworks, policy development, delivery of mandatory safeguarding training, leading complex safeguarding investigations, conducting regular audits, and providing specialist safeguarding supervision to the clinical team.

 - MDT Subject Matter Expert: Provide authoritative guidance to the Multi-Disciplinary Team (MDT) regarding complex mental health cases and the safe application of CBPMs.

 - Protocol Development: Use specialist insights to help refine mental health screening protocols and risk management frameworks


WHAT DO YOU NEED?

 - NMC/HCPC/GMC/GPhC valid and active registration.

 - Experience: A minimum of 5 years’ clinical experience, with a focus on mental health care, complex risk assessment, and longitudinal case management.

 - Safeguarding & Governance Expertise: Proven experience in clinical auditing, risk management, or safeguarding lead responsibilities within a healthcare setting.

 - Specialist Knowledge: A robust understanding of mental health medications, dual diagnosis, and the long term management of complex mental health patients.

 - Remote Proficiency: Confidence in using digital platforms for video consultations and maintaining accurate electronic patient records.

 - Communication: Exceptional organisational skills and a compassionate, patient-centred approach to managing complex clinical narratives.

source_jobs.title: Pharmacy Sales Manager
source_jobs.description:
Was beinhaltet die Position?

In der Position als Pharmacy Sales Manager übernehmst du die Verantwortung für die Weiterentwicklung und den Ausbau des Montu-Netzwerks im Segment der mittelgroßen Apotheken (Mid-Tier) in ganz Deutschland. Du kooperierst eng mit den Apotheken, erkennst Potenziale zur Steigerung der Produktakzeptanz und baust nachhaltige Partnerschaften auf, um unsere Kunden beim Wachstum im Bereich des medizinischen Cannabis zu unterstützen.

Diese Rolle kombiniert Tätigkeiten im Innen- und Außendienst und ist ideal für jemanden, der gerne Neugeschäft gewinnt, die Kundenperformance verbessert und messbare kommerzielle Ergebnisse erzielt.

 

Was werden deine Aufgaben sein?

Neukundengewinnung & Gebietsentwicklung

 - Identifizierung, Ansprache und Onboarding neuer Apotheken in deiner Region.

 - Aufbau einer starken, aktiven Pipeline und Konvertierung neuer Kundenpotenziale.

 - Souveräne Repräsentation von Montu sowohl in digitalen als auch in persönlichen Meetings.

Account Management

 - Verwaltung eines breiten Portfolios von B2B-Apothekenkunden (in der Regel 100–150 Accounts).

 - Steigerung der Bestellfrequenz, des Warenkorbwerts und des Produktmixes.

 - Durchführung von Produktberatungen zur Unterstützung von Apothekern und deren Teams.

Vertriebssteuerung (Sales Execution)

 - Förderung eines kontinuierlichen Umsatzwachstums in deinem Gebiet.

 - Kommunikation von Produkt-Insights, Umsatzpotenzialen und operativen Vorteilen.

 - Nachverfolgung von Performance-Kennzahlen und Berichterstattung der Fortschritte im CRM.

Zusammenarbeit

 - Enger Austausch mit den Teams Inside Sales, Key Account Management und dem Kundenservice, um eine flächendeckende Betreuung zu gewährleisten.

 - Weitergabe von lokalen Marktkenntnissen und Kundenfeedback an das Team.

 - Unterstützung bei Außendienst-Events, Produktschulungen und Aktivitäten zur Kundenbindung.

Außendiensttätigkeit

 - Durchführung regelmäßiger, gezielter Kundenbesuche mit klaren kommerziellen Zielen.

 - Kombination von Remote-Akquise mit wirkungsvollen Präsenzzeiten vor Ort.

Was bringst du mit?

 - Erfahrung im Vertrieb, Business Development oder Account Management.

 - Idealerweise ein Hintergrund im Gesundheitswesen, Pharma-Sektor, der Medizintechnik oder in einem dynamischen kommerziellen Umfeld.

 - Starke Kommunikations- und Beziehungsfähigkeiten.

 - Eine proaktive, zielorientierte Denkweise mit Fokus auf messbare Ergebnisse.

 - Fähigkeit, einen breiten Kundenstamm mit Struktur und Disziplin zu verwalten.

 - Sicherer Umgang mit CRM-Systemen und modernen digitalen Tools.

 - Deutsch auf Muttersprachniveau oder verhandlungssicher sowie solide Englischkenntnisse.

 - Gültiger Führerschein.

Warum Montu?

 - Gestalte die Zukunft des Gesundheitswesens in Deutschland durch innovative Telemedizin-Lösungen mit.

 - Werde Teil eines internationalen Teams mit einer kollaborativen Remote-First-Kultur.

 - Entfalte dich in einem schnell wachsenden Startup, in dem deine Arbeit direkten Einfluss hat.

 - Genieße Flexibilität, Autonomie und vielfältige Wachstumschancen.



Über uns

Wir bei Montu sind davon überzeugt, dass der Zugang zur Gesundheitsversorgung einfach und schnell sein und sich an den Menschen orientieren sollte – nicht an Systemen. Deshalb gestalten wir den Patientenweg neu: durch ein nahtloses, digital ausgerichtetes Erlebnis, das die Versorgung in greifbare Nähe rückt.

Unsere innovative Telemedizin-Plattform, unsere globale Reichweite und unser außergewöhnliches Portfolio an Medikamenten auf Cannabisbasis unterstützen Patienten, Ärzte und Apotheker, indem sie traditionelle Barrieren beseitigen und Menschen in die Lage versetzen, ihre Gesundheit selbstbewusst in die Hand zu nehmen.

In Zusammenarbeit mit Apotheken in ganz Deutschland stellen wir sicher, dass verschriebene Behandlungen schnell, sicher und reibungslos bereitgestellt werden – damit sich Patienten auf das Wesentliche konzentrieren können: sich besser zu fühlen

source_jobs.description:

We are seeking Permanent Consultant Psychiatrists to join our dynamic team. You will play a pivotal role in assessing and managing patients exploring medical cannabis treatment options. This role offers the opportunity to shape the future of medical cannabis treatment in the UK within a collaborative, multidisciplinary environment.

 - Willingness to participate in data collection and reporting

Why join us?

 - Highly Competitive Salary: £190,000 - £275,000 FTE, pro rata for part time roles

 - Flexible Working: Full time or part time options available

 - Cutting Edge Training: Access to CPD accredited medical cannabis education and specialist in house training from industry leaders

 - Collaborative Environment: Join a multidisciplinary team of healthcare professionals

 - Personal Growth: Be part of a pioneering field and shape the future of medical cannabis care in the UK

Training & Development:

Working arrangements:

Montu’s multidisciplinary model allows consultants to focus on prescribing and patient care, while nurses and pharmacists handle pre consultation and follow up tasks.

If you are passionate about innovative patient care, thrive in a dynamic environment, and are looking to make a real impact in the field of medical cannabis, we would love to hear from you!

Sessions:

Each standard session consists of 4 hours of patient consultations. Clinicians can choose their preferred working days and hours, with sessions spread flexibly if needed.

Appointments are 15 minutes in length for new and existing patients as Montu operates a multidisciplinary approach, leveraging a clinical skills mix where nurses and pharmacists handle pre-consultation and follow-up prescribing tasks allowing Consultants to focus on prescribing and patient care.

 

About MONTU

Montu UK is a leading digital health company specialising in cannabis based medicines (CBPM). source_jobs.title: General Manager & Site Director — Online Pharmacy
source_jobs.description:
The Opportunity



We are one of the UK's largest online pharmacies and the market leader in medical cannabis. Over the last few years, we have built a business that is growing at pace, transforming patient access to healthcare and setting new standards in a highly regulated industry.



Now we're looking for an exceptional operational leader to help take us through our next stage of growth.



This is not a role for someone who has simply inherited a large, well-established operation. We're looking for a builder. Someone who has taken a complex, high-volume fulfilment, logistics, dispensing, or operational function and scaled it dramatically. Someone who has redesigned processes, introduced automation, leveraged technology, and built teams capable of supporting rapid growth without compromising quality, compliance, or customer experience.



You'll play a pivotal role in shaping how our pharmacy operates as volumes continue to increase, identifying bottlenecks before they become problems, challenging the status quo, and creating systems that scale.



If you've led teams through significant growth, delivered operational transformation, and can point to tangible examples of making an operation faster, smarter, and more resilient, we'd love to hear from you.



You do not need to come from a pharmacy background. You do need to have successfully scaled something meaningful and have the ambition to do it again.



What You Will Own

 - Transitioning Montu from a fast-growing start-up into a nationally significant pharmacy operations and logistics business

 - Full end-to-end operational leadership - dispensing, fulfilment, patient communications, and clinical services

 - The technology and automation roadmap for the pharmacy operation - WMS, dispensing systems, workflow tools, carrier integrations. You will drive these decisions, not just partner on them

 - A 24/7 operation built for resilience - the systems, shift structures, and culture that mean performance does not depend on any one person

 - Commercial performance - cost per order, throughput, shrinkage, and contribution to growth

 - Building and leading a high-performing team of 150+ people, and developing the next layer of operational leadership beneath you

 - Regulatory and compliance accountability - GPhC, CQC, controlled drug frameworks - working closely with the Superintendent Pharmacist and Head of Pharmacy Operations

 - The patient experience from first contact to medication delivery - designed with the same rigour you would apply to any high-volume consumer operation



You must be able to demonstrate…

We will ask for specifics. Generalities will not be enough.

 - You have scaled a high-volume operation significantly - from early stage to genuine scale. You can talk in specifics about what you built, what broke, and how you fixed it

 - You have led teams of 150+ people in a fast-paced, shift-based environment

 - You have personally driven technology and automation decisions - not just been consulted on them. WMS selection, workflow redesign, dispensing or pick-pack tech, system integrations

 - You have operated in a regulated environment - pharmacy, pharmaceutical supply chain, healthcare, food safety, or equivalent - and you understand what compliance under pressure actually requires

 - You have a visible, on-the-floor leadership style. You are present, hands-on, and trusted by the people doing the work

 - You take commercial ownership seriously - you manage cost, track the metrics that matter, and make decisions as if it is your name on the door



This is not the right role if…

You have led large teams but not been hands-on redesigning workflows, implementing new systems, or personally solving fulfilment bottlenecks at pace. If your experience has been primarily inheriting and maintaining established operations rather than building and scaling them, this role will frustrate you - and you will frustrate us. source_jobs.title: Advanced Clinical Practitioner
source_jobs.description:
What is the job?

An Advanced Clinical Practitioner (ACP) working for Montu will practise within their advanced clinical competencies as part of a multi-disciplinary team (MDT), delivering high-quality patient-centred care in relation to Cannabis-Based Products for Medicinal use (CBPM).The ANP will provide comprehensive remote clinical assessments, structured consultations, medication reviews, and ongoing monitoring of patients prescribed CBPM. The role includes analysing complex clinical information to assess clinical need, independent prescribing, medicines optimisation, ensuring safe transfer of care, and contributing to systems that support safe and effective prescribing practices. This is a pivotal role focused on improving quality of care, patient safety, and operational efficiency. It requires strong clinical leadership, motivation, and a passion for delivering excellent, evidence-based care.

What will you be doing?

 - Conduct initial clinical consultations using video consultation to assess patients’ medical history, current treatment, and eligibility for CBPM treatment (training provided).

 - Undertake remote follow-up consultations for patients prescribed CBPM, providing ongoing monitoring, review, and optimisation of treatment plans.

 - Recognise and respond to changing clinical needs, identifying deterioration and taking appropriate clinical action and escalation where required.

 - Independently prescribe CBPM in line with national guidelines, clinical governance frameworks, and organisational policies.

 - Work collaboratively with consultants and other clinicians within the MDT to ensure safe, evidence-based prescribing and monitoring.

 - Support safe medicines management, including reviewing treatment efficacy, side effects, interactions, and risk factors.

 - Liaise closely with partner pharmacies to ensure accurate dispensing and timely delivery of medications.

 - Maintain accurate, contemporaneous electronic patient health records following consultations and prescribing decisions.

 - Act as a Level 3 Safeguarding practitioner, identifying, assessing, and escalating safeguarding concerns appropriately.

 - Implement prescribing changes in response to MHRA alerts, product withdrawals, and other relevant national or local guidance.

 - Participate in clinical audits to ensure safety, compliance, and continuous quality improvement.

 - Work alongside governance and operational teams to ensure compliance with CQC standards where medicines management is involved.

 - Contribute to service efficiency and sustainability through monitoring, audit, and quality improvement initiatives.

 - Attend clinical supervision sessions to support reflective practice and professional development.

 - Maintain all mandatory training and remain compliant with company policies, SOPs, and professional regulatory standards.

 - Attend work-related meetings as required.

 - Work flexibly on a shift basis where required.

 - Collaborate with the Clinical Leads and Head of Clinical Services and wider team to ensure KPIs and service targets are achieved.

 - Work closely with the operations team to ensure timely and effective patient care delivery.

What do you need?

 - Professional regulated core qualification with the NMC/HCPC

 - Qualified Advanced Nurse Practitioner (Master’s level or equivalent advanced practice qualification).

 - Independent Prescriber qualification (V300).

 - Minimum of 4 years post-qualification experience, with significant experience in advanced clinical assessment and prescribing.

 - Strong clinical assessment, diagnostic, and decision-making skills.

 - Evidence of continued professional development (CPD); completion of a CPD-accredited medical cannabis educational programme is desirable (training can be provided).

 - High level of professional accounta

Montu Pharmacy is the dispensing engine behind the UK's largest medical cannabis clinic. We are processing 50,000 prescriptions a month, growing 10% month on month, and we are not slowing down. Our pharmacy is entirely digital. Our patients are people who have often exhausted every other option. And the work we are doing here is genuinely changing lives.

We are looking for a Superintendent Pharmacist who does not need a traditional environment to thrive. Someone who has probably worked in a scale-up, a healthtech business, or an environment where the rulebook was still being written. Someone who is as comfortable in a conversation about automation and dispensing technology as they are in a GPhC inspection. You will own the professional and regulatory leadership of the pharmacy. You will sit alongside the Medical Director and Pharmacy General Manager, shape clinical strategy, and help define what the future of digital pharmacy actually looks like in practice. You will have scope that most Superintendent roles simply do not offer.

What this role is not: a comfortable, predictable, nine-to-five. The pace is real. The volume is significant. And the expectation is that you can hold rigorous professional standards whilst navigating growth, change, and complexity without needing everything to be settled before you act.

source_jobs.title: Hybrid Clinical Pharmacist - Winnersh
source_jobs.description:
What is the job?

Your patients have usually tried everything else. By the time they reach Montu UK, most have been through years of treatment that has not worked. You will be prescribing cannabis-based medicines to people for whom this is often a last resort, and for many of them it changes their quality of life substantially.

That is not a line. It is the reason the right pharmacist wants this job.

What will you be doing?

Your patients have usually tried everything else. By the time they reach Montu UK, most have been through years of treatment that has not worked. You will be prescribing cannabis-based medicines to people for whom this is often a last resort, and for many of them it changes their quality of life substantially.

 - Clinically review and prescribe CBMPs

 - Manage a complex patient caseload

 - Support the dispensary team with clinical queries

 - Contribute to education across patients and staff.

 - Work within a multidisciplinary clinical team

 - Maintain accurate patient records in line with GPhC standards.

What you need:

 - GPhC registered pharmacist with a current Independent Prescriber qualification

 - At least two years of prescribing experience

 - Experience in a clinical setting

 - Genuine interest in patient outcomes, not just the subject matter

 - Comfortable working at pace in an environment that is still evolving

 - CBPM knowledge is not required, we will train you properly



The honest bit:

Montu UK moves fast and things change. Processes are being built and improved all the time. If you need everything mapped out before you can start, this will frustrate you. If you thrive in a place that is growing and you want to help shape how it works, you will find a lot of room to do that here.

We are not looking for people who think prescribing cannabis sounds interesting. We are looking for people who care about the patient on the other end of the consultation, who will take a complex case seriously, and who want their work to matter.

This is not for you if

You want a stable, slow-moving clinical environment. You are drawn to this because it sounds progressive rather than because patient outcomes drive you. You need full process clarity before you can be effective. You are looking to coast on an IP qualification without continuing to develop.



What you get

 - A clinical role in a genuinely emerging field with real career development

 - Full CBPM training provided, you do not need to come in knowing this

 - A multidisciplinary team that takes the clinical work seriously

 - Hybrid working with a committed London base

 - The chance to be part of something that is still being built

What we offer:

 - Generous Leave: 25 days holiday (rising to 27 after year one and 30 after year three) + 8 bank holidays

 - Pension Matching: Up to 5% employer matching contributions

 - Private Healthcare Scheme

 - Flexibility and Wellness: Work-from-home options, cycle-to-work scheme, and more

 - Growth Opportunities: Collaborate across teams and represent Montu at events, with support to grow your skills and impact

 - Enhanced Maternity & Paternity Leave



About Us

Montu UK is a leading digital health company specialising in cannabis-based medicines (CBPM), dedicated to improving patient access to safe and effective treatments. Our mission is to transform lives by combining innovative technology with high-quality clinical care, ensuring patients receive the support they need at every step of their journey.



As a fast-growing organisation, we offer a collaborative and supportive environment where talented people can develop their careers while contributing to meaningful change in healthcare. At Montu UK, your work has a direct impact on improving patients’ lives and expanding access to modern medical treatments.

source_jobs.title: Senior Management Accountant
source_jobs.description:
What is the job?

We're looking for a Senior Management Accountant who wants to be part of scaling a local UK finance team to a 1st rate European finance team within a rapidly growing Pharmaceutical business.



At Montu, we're transforming patient access to medical cannabis across the UK, and our Finance team plays a critical role in making that happen. As we continue to grow within and beyond the UK, we're looking for someone who wants more than just a traditional accounting role. You'll have the opportunity to manage two team members and get involved in all things financial control as well as finance team transformation, continuous improvement, decision-making, training & mentoring and supporting our European expansion. You will help shape the future of the Finance function.



This is an excellent opportunity for a part-qualified, finalist or qualified accountant who wants exposure beyond the traditional management accounts cycle. You'll work closely with our Financial Controller and have access to our General Manager of Finance. You will gain visibility across the business, and have the opportunity to grow your career as we continue to scale.



If you're ambitious, curious, and looking for a role where your ideas are valued just as much as your technical skills, we'd love to hear from you.



What will you be doing?

 - Manage, mentor and develop the Accounts Payable Lead and Junior Accountant, supporting their professional growth and performance.

 - Ensure the smooth operation of finance processes through strong financial controls, accuracy and timely delivery.

 - Maintain and enhance internal control frameworks and ensure compliance with financial policies and procedures.

 - Actively learn and champion the adoption of existing and new systems and processes.

 - Identify opportunities to improve processes, automate manual tasks, and help us scale more effectively within finance and the wider business.

 - Act as a finance business partner to operational leaders, providing financial support, challenging assumptions and driving informed commercial decision-making.

 - Use your experience to mentor finance team members and the wider business on cost management and process improvements.

 - Perform detailed variance analysis against budget, forecast, and prior year and provide relevant commentary.

 - Own inventory accounting processes including stock reconciliations, valuation reviews and inventory-related financial controls.

 - Identify and mitigate financial risks.

 - Coordinate external audit requirements, ensure regulatory and tax compliance and support statutory accounts preparation.

 - Own balance sheet integrity through the review and sign-off of reconciliations and the timely investigation and resolution of reconciling items.

 - Own key month-end processes ensuring accurate and timely financial reporting.

 - Partner with the Finance Transformation Lead on system implementations and process improvements.

 - Partner with FP&A on the production of monthly management accounts and the delivery of management information.

 - Partner with FP&A on forecasting & budgeting rounds.

 - Partner with FP&A on cash flow forecasting and provide visibility on financial performance, working capital and business trends.
   
   

What do you need?

 - 5+ years' experience in management accounting, financial control or a similar finance role, with ownership of key finance processes and reporting activities.

 - Part-qualified, finalist or qualified ACCA, CIMA, ACA (or equivalent).

 - Strong understanding of accounting fundamentals and financial controls.

source_jobs.title: Clinical Pharmacist - Evenings and Weekends
source_jobs.description:
About the role:

We are recruiting a Clinical Pharmacist (Independent Prescriber) to join our London team. This is a hybrid role requiring 3 days on-site at our Kings Cross office and 2 days working remotely.

Please note: this role operates outside of standard office hours. On-site shifts run Monday to Friday 11am–8pm and weekends 8am–5pm, with a standard 40-hour working week. The role requires on-site working on 2 weekends per month, for which a day in lieu will be given. Flexibility to work evenings and weekends is essential and non-negotiable for this position.

As a Clinical Pharmacist (Independent Prescriber) you will split your time between three days on site and two days working remotely. You will provide safe, effective and personalised treatment, supporting patients with a wide range of needs.

Collaboration is central to this role. Working alongside a multidisciplinary team, you will help deliver outstanding patient care while also contributing to the development of clinical pathways. You will be involved in patient and staff education and play an active part in driving improvements in treatment outcomes within this rapidly growing field.

Key responsibilities:

As an On-Site Clinical Pharmacist, you will:

• Clinically check and review CBPM prescriptions for safety and accuracy.

• Prescribe medicines as an Independent Prescriber, tailoring treatments to individual patient needs.

• Provide expert guidance to prescribers and patients on treatment options.

• Maintain accurate patient records in line with GPhC standards.

• Support the dispensary team by handling complex clinical queries.

• Deliver education and training on CBPM to patients, clinicians, and staff.

• Collaborate with the wider team to streamline processes and improve outcomes.

 

What we are looking for:

 - GPhC registered pharmacist with an Independent Prescriber (IP) qualification

 - At least 2 years prescribing experience

 - Experience working in a clinical setting

 - Knowledge of CBPM is desired not essential or willingness to undergo specialised training

 - Clear and confident communicator across clinical and non-clinical settings

 - Highly effective at managing time and prioritising competing demands

 - Proactive and resourceful when identifying and resolving problems

 - Well-organised, detail-oriented, and able to manage complex workflows

 

What we offer:

 - Generous Leave: 25 days holiday (rising to 27 after year one and 30 after year three) + 8 bank holidays

 - Pension Matching: Up to 5% employer matching contributions

 - Private Healthcare Scheme

 - Flexibility and Wellness: Work-from-home options, cycle-to-work scheme, and more

 - Growth Opportunities: Collaborate across teams and represent Montu at events, with support to grow your skills and impact

 - Enhanced Maternity & Paternity Leave

Montu UK is a leading digital health company specialising in cannabis-based medicines (CBPM). source_jobs.title: Paramedic
source_jobs.description:
About the role

At Montu, the New Patient Paramedic sits at the very beginning of a patient's journey with us, and that first interaction matters more than most. The people who come to us have often spent years navigating a healthcare system that has not been able to give them answers. By the time they reach us, they are ready to try something different. Your role is to make that first step feel safe, considered, and human.

As a New Patient Paramedic you will conduct virtual assessments with patients across our core conditions, which span psychiatry, neurology, pain, and women's health. You will review medical histories, identify clinical needs, and determine whether cannabis-based treatment is appropriate, all within a multidisciplinary team that includes consultants, pharmacists, and specialist clinicians. This is not a passive gatekeeping role. You will be an active clinical voice, asking the right questions, spotting the things that need a closer look, and knowing when to refer on.

What makes this role different is the depth of the patient relationship you are able to build, even within a remote model. Because our patients return to us regularly throughout their treatment, you will see the impact of the decisions you make. When a patient tells you at their three week review that they are sleeping for the first time in years, or that their pain has reduced enough to let them get back to their life, you will know that your assessment was part of that outcome. That is what drives this team.

You will be joining a company that is growing fast and changing quickly. Things evolve here, processes improve, and no two weeks look exactly the same. If you are someone who thrives with variety, who wants to be part of building something rather than just maintaining it, and who believes that patients deserve better access to treatments that actually work, this is a role worth looking at.



What you will be doing:




VIRTUAL PATIENT ASSESSMENTS

 - Conduct comprehensive virtual assessments for new patients, reviewing medical histories, current symptoms, and identifying healthcare needs.

 - Work collaboratively with consultants and other clinicians as part of a multidisciplinary team (MDT) to support safe and effective patient care in line with clinical guidelines and scope of practice.

 - Provide clear, empathetic education about cannabis-based treatments, including potential benefits, side effects, contraindications, and usage guidance.

 - Maintain accurate, contemporaneous, and confidential records of all patient interactions using Montu’s electronic health record system.

 - Adhere to all Montu UK policies, healthcare regulations, professional standards, and patient confidentiality requirements.

 - Act as the first point of contact for new patients, responding to questions and concerns with professionalism, compassion, and clinical insight.

 - Maintain all mandatory training requirements and remain familiar with company policies, SOPs, and current clinical guidance.

What you need:

 - Strong clinical assessment, triage, and decision-making skills.

 - Excellent communication skills, particularly within virtual and remote consultation environments.

 - Empathetic, patient-centred approach with the ability to build rapport and trust remotely.

 - Proficiency in electronic health record systems and telehealth platforms.

 - Ability to work independently, prioritise workload effectively, and manage time efficiently in a remote setting.

 - Telemedicine or remote consultation experience is desirable.

 - Registered Paramedic qualification is essential.

 - Valid registration with the Health and Care Professions Council (HCPC).

 - Minimum of 5 years’ post-registration clinical experience as a paramedic.

source_jobs.title: Implementation and Change Lead
source_jobs.description:
IMPLEMENTATION & CHANGE LEAD

We’re looking for an ambitious and action-oriented Implementation & Change Lead to help accelerate Montu’s mission to transform healthcare through cannabis therapies. In this role, you’ll be an agent for change; managing and driving the delivery of projects that sit at the heart of scaling our healthtech ecosystem spanning clinical, pharmacy, digital, logistics, regulatory, and commercial initiatives.

This isn't a traditional project management role. We don't run a rigid methodology, and we're not looking for someone to impose one. We want someone who brings just enough structure to keep the important things on track, and who's as comfortable rolling up their sleeves as drawing up a plan. This is a hands-on role in a fast-paced scale-up. You’ll coordinate across teams, solve problems in real-time, and keep projects moving forward. At the same time, you’ll have the opportunity to shape how we deliver, building structure and momentum that helps Montu scale at pace.



What You'll Do

Find the work, not just manage it

 - Move through the organisation spotting what's broken, stuck, or about to become a problem and take ownership of fixing it, rather than waiting for it to land as a neatly-defined project.

 - Diagnose root causes, not just symptoms, and bring clarity to ambiguous or chaotic situations.

 - Make calls on what's worth doing, what can wait, and what to quietly kill.

Deliver, end to end

 - Take initiatives from messy idea to landed outcome; on time, on budget, to scope - working across Clinical, Pharmacy, Tech, Logistics, Legal and Commercial teams.

 - Bring just enough structure to keep delivery on track: A clear plan, named owners, critical-path, deadlines and visible progress, without drowning teams in process.

 - Introduce project management methodology, like Agile, where it helps maintain pace. Where it would slow things down, leave it out.

 - Track progress, manage risks, and unblock issues quickly to keep things moving.

Connect across the business

 - Build strong relationships with stakeholders, keeping them aligned and informed without burying everyone in meetings.

 - Bring people along on the journey: Make the case for change in plain language, win over sceptics rather than steamroll them and communicate clearly through the messy middle so new ways of working stick.

Make us better as we grow

 - Leave the processes you touch faster and simpler than you found them.

 - Build the delivery muscle of the wider team — and, over time, potentially a small team of your own.

 - Champion delivery best practices (like Agile and Lean Six Sigma), helping the team work smarter as we grow.

 - Optimise processes by applying Lean Six Sigma and continuous improvement principles to make our operations faster, smarter, and ready to scale.

 - Get your hands dirty and bring a pragmatic, hands-on approach to getting things done in a fast-paced, sometimes scrappy scale-up environment.



What You’ll Bring

We're looking for an Implementation & Change Lead who runs towards those problems: someone who can walk into a messy, half-formed situation, work out what actually needs to happen and make it happen, often before anyone's asked.

 - A genuine bias for action and a high degree of autonomy; you find the work, set your own direction, and don't wait to be told what to fix.

 - A doer as much as a planner: You don't just oversee projects, you get your hands dirty to deliver them.

 - A track record of bringing order to chaos in fast-moving, ambiguous environments.

 - A strong, enthusiastic and strategic change communicator who tailors the message to any audience, makes a compelling case for why, and brings people on board rather than just informing them.

 - Strong grasp of Agile and hybrid delivery models, and the ability to adapt them pragmatically. Certifications (Agile, PMP, PRINCE2, Lean Six Sigma) are nice-to-have but mindset matters more.

 - Comfortable working with diverse stakehold

You will support our pharmacy operation at the Winnersh site. You are responsible for making sure every medicine that leaves our pharmacy is right. That means accuracy checking dispensed medicines, keeping standards high, and actively contributing to a culture where getting it right the first time is non-negotiable. You will not be managed through every task. You will be trusted to lead, to spot problems before they become incidents, and to raise the bar for everyone around you.

 - Carrying out accuracy checks on dispensed medicines, ensuring full compliance with legal, operational and safety standards

 - Taking ownership of the end-to-end dispensing process — not just your part of it

 - Maintaining clear, accurate prescription records and escalating discrepancies without hesitation

 - Actively mentoring junior colleagues and helping to build a team culture rooted in accountability, safety and quality

 - Staying sharp on professional development, best practice and regulatory changes — because you want to, not because you have to

 - Working with pace and purpose alongside the wider pharmacy team to ensure every patient gets exactly what they need, when they need it

What do you need?

 - Level 3 NVQ in Pharmacy Services (ACT) or equivalent ACT qualification

 - Solid, proven experience in a pharmacy setting with a strong track record in accuracy checking

 - The kind of attention to detail that you simply cannot switch off
